publication_contract.review_policy: Scheduled runs publish GitHub Pages and upload the benchmark-leaderboard artifact for maintainer review; they do not commit generated benchmark artifacts back to main.
publication_contract.claim_policy: Dashboard rows may publish checked-in benchmark evidence; remote, managed-serverless, 50M, and 100M production claims stay locked until strict evidence artifacts pass.

scenario.name: agent_coherence
scenario.description: Agent task simulation over long user history. It measures whether retrieved memory lets an agent choose the correct personalized action while suppressing stale facts and saving context tokens.

agent_impact.claim_boundary: Agent-impact rows come from checked-in benchmark artifacts. They show behavioral lift on the configured tasks; they do not claim general agent success outside the listed scenarios.

structured_memory.claim_boundary: Structured-memory rows come from the checked-in scale-readiness artifact. They prove typed payload routing, provenance, persistence, temporal recall, and graph traversal on the deterministic fixture; they do not claim full production multimodal model quality.

structured_contract.claim_boundary: Structured-memory rows come from the checked-in scale-readiness artifact. They prove typed payload routing, provenance, persistence, temporal recall, and graph traversal on the deterministic fixture; they do not claim full production multimodal model quality.

required_evidence.title: External multimodal encoder and object-store benchmark
required_evidence.claim_unlocked: Production multimodal encoder quality, cross-modal recall, object-store persistence, and latency SLO.

memory_os_intelligence.claim_boundary: Memory OS intelligence rows come from checked-in deterministic scale, agent-coherence, staging canary, and admission artifacts. They prove worker behavior, policy generation, cache prewarm, predictive prefetch, priority learning, adaptive forgetting, consolidation, and rollout safety on these fixtures. They do not unlock unattended production Memory OS automation until the admission gate is admitted with real shared Redis, distributed lock, runtime env, and large-scale evidence.

cluster_autoscale.claim_boundary: Cluster autoscale evidence is extracted from the checked-in scale-readiness artifact. It proves deterministic shard placement, failure-domain availability, autoscale planning, rebalance planning, operator reconciliation, quorum safety, active-active convergence, field-state CRDT behavior, and the 100M capacity envelope on these fixtures. It is not a real 100M vector-query latency benchmark, managed Kubernetes production run, or independent multi-region SLO.

action_required.title: External HTTP service-node load
issues: environment must be a real remote/staging/production deployment
source must identify a real remote run, not a sample or loopback

action_required.title: Managed/serverless remote telemetry
action_required.claim_unlocked: Hosted/serverless p99, cold-start, error-rate, and scale-out SLO.

action_required.title: 10M sharded Qdrant service load
action_required.claim_unlocked: Horizontally sharded Qdrant service recall/latency SLO.

action_required.title: 100M remote load result
action_required.claim_unlocked: 100M+ memories with measured sharded-Qdrant recall, p99, and cost SLO.

cost_efficiency.claim_boundary: Measured rows come from checked-in load artifacts. Planned rows are capacity and cost contracts only; they do not unlock production latency or recall claims until the matching benchmark result exists.

jobs.title: External HTTP service-node load
issues: cluster node requires at least 4 URLs
warnings: WAVEMIND_API_KEY is not set; only use this if the target endpoints are intentionally unauthenticated
jobs.safe_launch_command: gh workflow run external-http-cluster-load.yml -f nodes="$WAVEMIND_CLUSTER_NODES" -f nodes_manifest_json="$WAVEMIND_CLUSTER_NODES_MANIFEST_JSON" -f namespace_count="32" -f memories_per_namespace="8" -f workers="8" -f batch_query_size="24" -f replication_factor="3" -f read_quorum="1" -f read_fanout="1" -f p99_slo_ms="1000" -f commit_results="false"
jobs.publish_launch_command: gh workflow run external-http-cluster-load.yml -f nodes="$WAVEMIND_CLUSTER_NODES" -f nodes_manifest_json="$WAVEMIND_CLUSTER_NODES_MANIFEST_JSON" -f namespace_count="32" -f memories_per_namespace="8" -f workers="8" -f batch_query_size="24" -f replication_factor="3" -f read_quorum="1" -f read_fanout="1" -f p99_slo_ms="1000" -f commit_results="true"

requirements.action: Resolve execution_plan.blocked_task_ids before scheduling Memory OS.

requirements.title: All Memory OS worker lanes are planned
requirements.evidence: adaptive-forgetting, architecture-advice, cache-prewarm, consolidation, maintenance, memory-os, predictive-prefetch
requirements.action: Planner must include memory-os, cache-prewarm, predictive-prefetch, forgetting, consolidation, maintenance, and architecture-advice.

requirements.title: Query audit traffic enables prewarm and predictive workers
requirements.action: Enable audited query traffic in staging, replay representative traffic, then rerun memory-os-admission.

requirements.title: Consolidation worker is active when clusters exist
requirements.action: Seed enough representative memories/query traffic for stable concept clusters before production rollout.

requirements.title: Shared Redis cache is configured when the plan requires it
requirements.action: Set WAVEMIND_REDIS_URL or pass --redis-url before enabling multi-worker Memory OS.

requirements.title: Distributed single-flight lock is configured for state mutation
requirements.action: Set WAVEMIND_MEMORY_OS_LOCK_REDIS_URL or pass --lock-redis-url before running production Memory OS workers.

requirements.title: State-mutating tasks are singleton/idempotent
requirements.action: Keep mutation tasks as cluster singletons with idempotency keys.

requirements.title: Policy manifest covers prefetch, priority, forgetting, consolidation, scale, and coordination
requirements.evidence: consolidation-policy, coordination-policy, forgetting-policy, prefetch-policy, priority-policy, scale-policy
requirements.action: Memory OS admission requires a full policy manifest before rollout.

requirements.title: Production infrastructure contract is explicit
requirements.evidence: OpenTelemetry metrics for worker duration, errors, and warmed queries, Redis-compatible shared hot-query cache, distributed worker lock or single-flight scheduler, durable queue or Kubernetes CronJobs
requirements.action: Production plans must list Redis, lock/scheduler, queue/CronJob, and OpenTelemetry requirements.

requirements.title: Large target stays behind strict architecture evidence
requirements.action: Do not admit Memory OS production rollout for million-plus targets until architecture-required evidence is resolved.

requirements.title: Required runtime environment is present
requirements.action: Provide every required environment variable before scheduling the worker set.

memory_os_canary.claim_boundary: staging_canary; not remote Kubernetes, Redis, or 10M production evidence

checks.title: Representative query audit created hot queries
checks.action: Replay representative staging traffic with query audit enabled before Memory OS rollout.

checks.title: Memory OS prewarmed hot recall paths
checks.action: Fix cache wiring or audit filters before scheduling cache-prewarm workers.

checks.title: Memory OS generated predictive follow-up queries
checks.action: Keep transition audit enabled so predictive prefetch can learn follow-up paths.

checks.title: Memory OS applied bounded priority learning
checks.action: Investigate recall quality if hot queries do not produce priority updates.

checks.title: Memory OS purged expired stale memories
checks.action: Keep maintenance enabled before stale TTL records can compete with live memories.

checks.title: Memory OS scheduler passes staging admission
checks.action: Resolve admission blockers before enabling production automation.
